articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Vecino pounces to hand South Korea first loss under new boss Klinsmann
article.description: Matias Vecino scored from close range in the second half as Uruguay beat South Korea to hand Jurgen Klinsmann his first loss as their coach.
article.linkText: Uruguay loss keeps Klinsmann winless with S. Korea

images.name: Klinsmann South Korea Uruguay_230328 [1296x729]

article.story:
Matias Vecino scored from close range in the second half as Uruguay earned a 2-1 win over South Korea in Seoul on Tuesday, handing Jurgen Klinsmann his first defeat in his second game at the helm of the Taeguk Warriors.

The former Germany and United States coach was appointed last month to replace Paulo Bento and has not won in either of his first two games in charge having seen his side draw 2-2 with Colombia on Friday.

Defender Sebastian Coates gave the Uruguayans the lead in the 10th minute when he rose above the Korean defence to head in Federico Valverde's corner.

But Hwang In-beom levelled six minutes after the interval when, with the Uruguayan defence in disarray, Lee Ki-je cut the ball back for the midfielder to slot in.

Vecino restored his side's lead in the 63rd minute when he pounced on a loose ball in the six-yard box after Jo Hyeon-woo had parried Joaquin Piquerez's free kick from outside the area.

The Koreans thought they had equalised again when Kim Young-gwon rose above goalkeeper Santiago Mele to head in with 15 minutes remaining, only for referee Yusuke Araki to judge the defender had handled the ball after a lengthy VAR check.

Celtic striker Oh Hyeon-gyu then fell victim of a marginal offside call when he thought he had levelled seven minutes from time as the Uruguayans clung on.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Korea Republic 0, Uruguay 1. Sebastián Coates (Uruguay) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Federico Valverde with a cross   following a corner.
Substitution, Korea Republic. Son Jun-Ho replaces Jung Woo-Young because of an injury.
First Half ends, Korea Republic 0, Uruguay 1.
Second Half begins Korea Republic 0, Uruguay 1.
Substitution, Uruguay. Felipe Carballo replaces Manuel Ugarte.
Goal!  Korea Republic 1, Uruguay 1. Hwang In-Beom (Korea Republic) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Lee Ki-Je.
Substitution, Uruguay. Matías Arezo replaces Maximiliano Gómez.
Substitution, Uruguay. Agustín Canobbio replaces Facundo Torres.
Goal!  Korea Republic 1, Uruguay 2. Matías Vecino (Uruguay) left footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner    following a set piece situation.
Substitution, Korea Republic. Oh Hyeon-Gyu replaces Hwang Ui-Jo.
Lee Kang-In (Korea Republic) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Sebastián Coates (Uruguay) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Uruguay. Matías Viña replaces Jonathan Rodríguez.
Substitution, Korea Republic. Cho Gue-Sung replaces Lee Jae-Sung.
Second Half ends, Korea Republic 1, Uruguay 2.
